Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.content: The transitions query returned no output, which means no transitions had `gameplay_changed=True`. This is strange. Let me check the transitions more carefully to see what actions have been taken and what their results were.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check all transitions
print(f"Total transitions: {len(transitions)}")
for i, t in enumerate(transitions[-10:]):
    result = t.result
    gc = result.get('gameplay_changed', 'N/A')
    bc = result.get('board_changed', 'N/A')
    ni = result.get('no_impact', 'N/A')
    print(f"  T{i}: {t.action} gameplay={gc} board={bc} no_impact={ni}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking on the purple circle at (18, 57) to move the orange object up the trail
# First, let me verify the purple circle's position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
purple_circle_pixels = []
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'p' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        cr = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        purple_circle_pixels.append((cr, cc))
print(f"Purple circle pixels: {purple_circle_pixels}")

# The purple circle is around (18, 57)
# Let me click on it
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 18, 'col': 57}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
